title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: DHS Reveals Troubling New Details After Mexican Immigrant Killed In ICE Raid
stories.summary:
DHS Reveals Troubling New Details After Mexican Immigrant Killed In ICE Raid. Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, the Mexican immigrant killed on Tuesday by federal immigration agents in Texas, was not the target of an ICE investigation, according to a Department of Homeland Security spokesman.

The agents were searching for two people from Guatemala, whom the agents believed were in the van that Araujo was driving. Those individuals were ...

BBC: Man fatally shot by ICE in Houston was not intended target, DHS says. BBC reports that immigration agents were looking for a different person when they shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo during a traffic stop, officials say.

stories.title: China takes a page from SpaceX and recaptures the first stage of a rocket to reuse it
stories.summary:
China takes a page from SpaceX and recaptures the first stage of a rocket to reuse it. China successfully recaptured the first stage of a rocket after a launch on Friday in a breakthrough for the country's space program, state media said.

BBC: China lands reusable rocket for first time, state media says.

BBC reports that it follows similar landings of reusable rockets by US-owned companies SpaceX and Blue Origin.

stories.title: Trump refuses to sign bipartisan housing bill, urges lawmakers to focus on SAVE America Act
stories.summary:
Trump refuses to sign bipartisan housing bill, urges lawmakers to focus on SAVE America Act. President Trump said he won't sign a bipartisan housing bill and pressed lawmakers to shift their focus to the SAVE America Act, a voter-ID bill that he has pushed the Senate to pass.

Al Jazeera: Trump refuses to sign US housing bill over voting act standoff.

Al Jazeera reports that the housing legislation will become US law at midnight with or without President Donald Trump's signature.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies
stories.summary:
Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies. The attacks appear to be the latest phase of Ukraine's bid to choke off supplies and routes into and out of occupied Crimea.

Al Jazeera: Ukraine chokes fuel to Crimea, Russian consumers, targeting military supply.

Al Jazeera reports that ukraine’s unmanned systems have cut overland routes and begun to target Russian shadow tankers.

stories.title: East Asia braces for destructive typhoon as landslides kill 15 in Philippines
stories.summary:
East Asia braces for destructive typhoon as landslides kill 15 in Philippines. Heading for Taiwan and south-eastern China, the 1,000 km-wide Bavi is forecast to be one of the strongest storms in decades.

Al Jazeera: Philippines landslides kill 15 as Typhoon Bavi threatens region.

Al Jazeera reports that typhoon Bavi is intensifying seasonal monsoon rains across the region as it moves towards Taiwan and China.

stories.title: Trump Team Drops The Hammer After Tim Walz’s Illegal Alien Pardon
stories.summary:
Trump Team Drops The Hammer After Tim Walz’s Illegal Alien Pardon. The Trump administration has swooped in to deport an illegal immigrant child sex offender weeks after Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz pardoned him.

Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced Friday that he revoked the legal status of Laotian national Tou Lue Vang, resulting in his deportation by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The Minnesota Board of ...

Washington Times: Trump admin deports criminal migrant pardoned by Tim Walz. Washington Times reports that the Trump administration said it has stripped legal status and deported a migrant whom Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz tried to protect from ouster by granting him a pardon for sexually abusing a 10-year-old girl.

stories.title: Mamdani’s Tribute To New York Immigrants Has A Few Exceptions
stories.summary:
Mamdani’s Tribute To New York Immigrants Has A Few Exceptions. New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani highlighted thirty immigrant neighborhoods in a recent post — and caught immediate backlash because he failed to mention Little Italy.

The famous Italian neighborhood in Lower Manhattan has been a cultural landmark since the 1880s — meaning that it was established long before many of the other neighborhoods Mamdani ... Breitbart: Mamdani 'Immigrant Map' Omits Irish, Italian, and Jewish Contributions to New York City.

Breitbart reports that mayor Zohran Mamdani is taking heat for a map celebrating the city's "immigrant enclaves" that omits Italian, Irish, and Jewish neighborhoods. The post Mamdani ‘Immigrant Map’ Omits Irish, Italian, and Jewish Contributions to New York City appeared first on Breitbart .

stories.title: The brown huntsman timed as the fastest spider at a top speed of 3.59 m/s – video
stories.summary:
The brown huntsman timed as the fastest spider at a top speed of 3.59 m/s – video. Brown huntsman spiders were filmed to measure their speed.

The 2021 research has been included in new analysis of the speeds of more than 250 spider species by scientists in the UK and Germany, which concludes the brown huntsman, Heteropoda jugulans, has a top speed of 3.59 m/s, making it the fastest of all spiders measured.

The study includes research supervised by Dr Christofer Clemente, an evolutionary biomechanist at the University of the Sunshine Coast in Queensland. ABC News: Australia's huntsman fastest spider on record, new study suggests.

ABC News reports that a species of huntsman spider found in Queensland is the fastest mover out of more than 250 spider species analysed in a new study.

links.headline: The brown huntsman timed as the fastest spider at a top speed of 3.59 m/s – video
links.excerpt: Brown huntsman spiders were filmed to measure their speed. The 2021 research has been included in new analysis of the speeds of more than 250 spider species by scientists in the UK and Germany, which concludes the brown huntsman, Heteropoda jugulans, has a top speed of 3.59 m/s, making it the fastest of all spiders measured. The study includes research supervised by Dr Christofer Clemente, an evolutionary biomechanist at the University of the Sunshine Coast in Queensland
links.imageAlt: The brown huntsman timed as the fastest spider at a top speed of 3.59 m/s – video

stories.title: Anika Wells says Telstra must 'face the music' as regulator begins investigation – video
stories.summary:
Anika Wells says Telstra must 'face the music' as regulator begins investigation – video. The communications minister, Anika Wells, says Telstra must 'face the music' and rebuild public trust after its nationwide outage, vowing that the government will hold the telco to account.

She says Telstra should provide an expedited process for customers seeking compensation.

The Australian Communications and Media Authority's deputy chair Adam Suckling says the regulator is investigating the outage. Suckling says under laws related to the triple zero system, Telstra can face civil penaltie.

links.excerpt: The communications minister, Anika Wells, says Telstra must 'face the music' and rebuild public trust after its nationwide outage, vowing that the government will hold the telco to account. She says Telstra should provide an expedited process for customers seeking compensation. The Australian Communications and Media Authority's deputy chair Adam Suckling says the regulator is investigating the outage. Suckling says under laws related to the triple zero system, Telstra can face civil penaltie

stories.title: EU accuses Meta of failing to tackle mental health risks of ‘addictive design’
stories.summary:
EU accuses Meta of failing to tackle mental health risks of ‘addictive design’. Regulators say Facebook and Instagram features such as autoplay and infinite scroll contribute to ‘compulsive use’ EU regulators have accused Meta, the company behind Facebook and Instagram, of failing to tackle the risks of its “addictive design” on the physical and mental health of users.

In an official charge sheet against Meta released on Friday, the European Commission said features such as video autoplay and infinite scroll, which provides an endless stream of content, “shif. TechCrunch: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram.

TechCrunch reports that the tech giant is in breach of the Digital Services Act by focusing on features like infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and the highly personalized recommendation algorithms, the European Commission said

stories.title: Bank of England handed powers to regulate key tech firms including Amazon and Google
stories.summary:
Bank of England handed powers to regulate key tech firms including Amazon and Google.

Direct oversight of ‘critical third parties’ such as Oracle and Microsoft given to ensure resilient cyber-defences and help safeguard UK economy The Bank of England has been handed powers to regulate important tech firms including Amazon and Google from next week, amid fears that system failures could threaten financial stability and harm consumers.

stories.title: Alarm over launch of facial recognition in UK shops that instantly alerts police
stories.summary:
Alarm over launch of facial recognition in UK shops that instantly alerts police.

Civil liberties groups say Facewatch system in stores such as Sainsbury’s and B&M is ‘dangerous escalation’ Facial recognition technology in shops will soon alert police in real time to the presence of serious offenders, with civil liberties groups warning of a “dangerous escalation” towards surveillance and criminalisation in the retail sector.

Facewatch, a facial recognition system used by more than 100 businesses including Sainsbury’s, B&M and Spar to monitor thieves, said it w.

stories.title: DepEd boosts oversight as 168 LGUs push classroom construction projects
stories.summary:
DepEd boosts oversight as 168 LGUs push classroom construction projects.

MANILA, Philippines — Building on the Department of Education’s (DepEd) commitment to transparency and rigorous fiscal standards, Education Secretary Sonny Angara has reinforced institutional oversight of the nationwide localized classroom building program.

To date, DepEd is actively managing partnerships with 168 local government units (LGUs) that have signed Supplemental Memoranda of Agreement (MOA), encompassing 72 provinces,.

stories.title: DepEd: Over 6,100 schools suspend classes due to Inday
stories.summary:
DepEd: Over 6,100 schools suspend classes due to Inday.

ILOILO CITY — Over 6,100 public schools across 11 regions have suspended face-to-face classes and shifted to Flexible Learning Options (FLO) as Typhoon Inday and the enhanced southwest monsoon continue to disrupt education and threaten communities nationwide, according to the Department of Education (DepEd).

In its Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Service (DRRMS) situation report.

stories.summary:

MANILA, Philippines – Jaden and Friends Inc., the nonprofit known for supporting Filipino children living with congenital heart disease and developmental needs, recently received a ₱7.776-million grant for its Developmental Therapy Support Program from Jonathan M.

Sterling. For overseas Filipino worker Janice Lopez, saving her son Jaden meant leaving the country to find the people.

stories.title: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed
stories.summary: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed. HyperTexting's new app aims to make the open web feel more like social media by turning websites, blogs, newsletters, and podcasts into a scrollable feed, while also making it easier to post to your own website.

stories.title: Reeves to launch City ‘skills compact’ committing firms to retrain staff in AI
stories.summary:
Reeves to launch City ‘skills compact’ committing firms to retrain staff in AI.

Exclusive: Plan to improve skills of thousands of financial sector workers to keep pace with tech revolution Chancellor Rachel Reeves is to announce a new City “skills compact” that will commit firms such as Barclays and Lloyds to retraining thousands of financial sector workers for the AI revolution.

The financial services skills compact will be launched on Tuesday, during what is likely to be Reeves’s final Mansion House speech to City bosses before Andy Burnham’s expected takeo.

stories.title: Pokémon Go fans gather in Times Square to celebrate 10 years of game – video
stories.summary:
